title: T1 Engine: per-worker git worktrees — make pool>1 safe for editing tickets
requested_by: david (factory-console session — 'run more than one ticket at a time')
prompt: Read and execute the war game at backlog/wargames/T1-19-worktree-per-worker-isolation.md move by move. Give each warm worker its own git worktree (isolate code edits) while the engine store stays the single shared coordination substrate reached by absolute path; consolidate each worker's commit onto main at reap; clean worktrees up on teardown. Make Move 0's isolation decision (worktree vs clone) explicitly and record it. Prove with the isolated two-worktree test; DEFER the live pool=2 proof to an attended run (it reboots the pool — T1-06 self-hosting class). On abort, park to needs-decision/.
verify: warm_pool.py + orchestrator.py parse; git-worktree add/remove present in warm_pool.py; .worktrees/ gitignored; two hand-made worktrees each editing the same file consolidate to main with both changes and a clean tree; pool=1 dispatch reaps exactly as before and the shared store is read/written from a worktree cwd.
